body.content:
{{ template | label: 'Set your Zendesk subdomain.', description: 'Replace every instance of [YOUR_SUBDOMAIN] in the Prompt and Instructions with your Zendesk subdomain (the part before .zendesk.com in your URL).', default: 'Each week, review all support tickets from the past 7 days. Include the dates of the tickets you''re reviewing at the top of the summary. Summarize the top 2-3 issues customers faced or notable trends based on the actual content of customer messages-what users are trying to do, where they''re confused, or what they''re asking for help with. For each trend: - Describe the specific issue or user request in plain terms - Estimate how many tickets show this trend - Be specific (e.g., "confusion about adding a delay step after order fulfillment"), not general (e.g., "workflow questions"). - Skip ticket metadata like submission method or timestamps. Focus only on user intent and problem content. IMPORTANT--LINKS: - Provide Zendesk agent ticket links (not API links). - These should look like: https://[YOUR_SUBDOMAIN].zendesk.com/agent/tickets/ - Avoid API links like https://[YOUR_SUBDOMAIN].zendesk.com/api/v2/tickets/.json which don''t work without an API token. Also include 2-3 practical tips for the product team based on the trends you''ve observed. IMPORTANT - OUTPUT FORMAT: Output the entire summary as valid HTML for a rich-text email. Do NOT use Markdown syntax (no #, ##, ####, -, or ** characters). Use HTML tags only. Separate every block with proper tags so nothing runs together into one paragraph. Start the summary with exactly this structure:

Support Weekly Summary

Review Dates: [start date] to [end date]

' }}
body.instructions:
{{ template | label: 'Set your Zendesk subdomain.', description: 'Replace every instance of [YOUR_SUBDOMAIN] in the Prompt and Instructions with your Zendesk subdomain (the part before .zendesk.com in your URL).', default: 'Then include three major sections in this order, using

headings:

Top Customer Issues and Trends

Practical Tips for the Product Team

Use HTML tags throughout. Do NOT use Markdown syntax (no #, ##, ####, -, or **). In "Top Customer Issues and Trends," include 2–3 main themes. Each theme must have: - A short, specific title as an

heading (e.g.,

1. Issue Title

) - A Description in its own

, with the word "Description:" wrapped in , explaining the issue or user request - A Ticket Estimate in its own

using (including the approximate count), followed by a

    with one
  • per ticket - Each link formatted as Descriptive Title - Every href must start with https://[YOUR_SUBDOMAIN].zendesk.com/agent/tickets/ - Avoid API URLs (nothing beginning with https://[YOUR_SUBDOMAIN].zendesk.com/api/...) - Skip all ticket metadata (channel, assignee, requester, tags, etc.) - Focus only on customer messages and intent — what users are trying to do, where they''re confused, or what they''re asking for help with In "Practical Tips for the Product Team," include 2–3 short, actionable recommendations based on the trends. Each tip is its own

    with a bolded short title () followed by a one-line explanation. Be concise, plainspoken, and specific (e.g., "Confusion about adding a delay step after order fulfillment" instead of "workflow questions"). Maintain a professional and informative tone throughout.' }}

body.to: {{ template | label: 'Email address', description: 'Where should we send the weekly report?' }}
body.subject: Last Week's Zendesk Conversations
